stem: A pupil has a list of integers stored in a variable called numbers and wants to know how many of them are even.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Accept OCR pseudocode or Python. Look for: counter initialised to 0, loop over the list, evenness test (`% 2 == 0` or equivalent), increment, output the result.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write a program in OCR pseudocode or Python that counts how many of the values in the list `numbers` are even and prints the count.

mark_points.text: counter initialised to 0 before the loop
tests for even (`% 2 == 0` or MOD 2 == 0) and increments the counter

misconceptions.label: Single-element check
misconceptions.description: Programs that test only one value (no loop) cannot reach the count-many-evens behaviour.
